reasoning: While programmatic signals show excellent content preservation (96.7% recall) and perfect table/image counts, the LLM fidelity score of 0.6 falls into the weak quality band and includes null values for structural and content scores. This discrepancy between high programmatic stats and a low, incomplete fidelity signal on a large document (43k words) with complex visual elements (shading, colored text) triggers a mandatory human review under the 3x cost-asymmetry rule.
action_items: Verify that the shading and colored text have been converted to accessible HTML without loss of legibility.
Review a sample of the tables to ensure structural integrity was maintained, as programmatic signals only confirm count consistency.
Validate that no content was lost in the gaps where LLM fidelity scoring failed.
